column4: A visual novel about a 3 year old girl and a dog, who got lost in the forest of siberian wilderness.
An audio-based art project that tries to hold on to the everyday sounds of Kashmir.
A narrative game and a small window into nomadic life on the steppe, so the people a thousand years from now can see how their ancestors once lived with the land.
A replica of a desk from the past, full of small memories and small activities. A reminder that taking time for yourself is important, and that rest is not something to feel guilty about.
A personal interactive piece inspired by my experience with ice skating, blending creativity, and a love for internet culture.

A made up memory of a virtual Newjeans concert page: which is a shared memory that lives amongst Bunnies (fandom). This project is a reminder that even in digital spaces can hold that feeling — you were part of the moment.
This project shows the slow decay of urban heritage. I used pixels to represent the way we remember places that are physically disappearing.
This project temps to explore how language deteriorates and transforms. Where meaning may not survive over time, and even when reconstructed, it would still remain incomplete.
An interactive digital tree that grows when users take care of it. Small choices matter. what you do today shapes what grows tomorrow
